article_title: Esports World Cup 2026 Sets Record With $75 Million Prize Pool Across 24 Games

content_preview.title: Esports World Cup 2026 Sets Record With $75 Million Prize Pool Across 24 Games
content_preview.summary: The Esports World Cup Foundation has announced a $75 million total prize pool for its 2026 event in Riyadh, a 15 percent increase over the previous year, with more than 2,000 players from over 100 countries competing across 24 games from July 6 through August 23.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Esports World Cup Foundation has unveiled the full details for its 2026 event, confirming a record-breaking $75 million total prize pool that eclipses the $65 million offered in 2025. Scheduled to run from July 6 through August 23 in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, the seven-week tournament will host more than 2,000 players representing 200 clubs from over 100 countries across 25 tournaments spanning 24 games, according to the [official announcement distributed via PR Newswire](https://ww...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.source_verification: PR Newswire press release confirms all key claims: $75M prize pool, 24 games, 25 tournaments, 2000+ players, 200+ clubs, 100+ countries, July 6-August 23, Riyadh, $30M club championship, CEO Ralf Reichert quote, 2025 attendance (3M visitors, 750M viewers).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All figures verified. Note: article says 15% increase ($65M to $75M) which is actually 15.4% — acceptable rounding. Game lineup additions (Fortnite, Trackmania) and returning titles confirmed.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral event reporting. Appropriately includes the geopolitical context of Saudi Arabia's sportswashing debate without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: Solid event news coverage with good contextualization of Saudi Arabia's esports strategy and competitive landscape.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Well-sourced esports news at 656 words. Primary source is official press release, supplemented by Morningstar and Xinhua coverage.
